result:
## Summary

The site had pivoted its product (InboxZap, an email cleanup tool → Lumina, an AI meeting assistant) without updating the SEO surface, which was actively suppressing organic traffic. Fixed:

1. **Brand/metadata mismatch** — `<title>`/description/OG tags said "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes" while every visible page said Lumina. Search snippets contradicted page content, tanking CTR and trust signals. Rewrote all metadata to match, added `metadataBase`, canonical URLs, Twitter card, and `SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD.
2. **Broken blog (highest-impact fix)** — `/blog` was a client component fetching `/api/posts`, an endpoint that doesn't exist. Every visitor and Googlebot saw "Could not load posts." This explains the terrible positions (42–58) on the 12 post URLs GSC still shows impressions for. Rebuilt as SSR/SSG with real per-slug routes (`/blog/[slug]`), unique title/description/canonical/`BlogPosting` JSON-LD per post, and content repositioned to Lumina's actual ICP (meeting notes, action items, async recaps) instead of the old email-cleanup topics that no longer match the product.
3. **404'd nav links** — `/features` and `/pricing` were linked in the nav and every CTA but didn't exist. Built both out with real content and metadata.
4. **Dead-end FAQ redirect** — `/faq` 301-redirected to `/#faq`, a fragment that didn't exist on the homepage. Added a real FAQ section (including a "what happened to InboxZap" entry to catch confused legacy-brand searchers) with `FAQPage` schema.
5. **Sitemap gap** — sitemap explicitly excluded blog posts (TODO comment). Now includes all 12 post URLs plus features/pricing.
6. **Broken OG image** — referenced a PNG that didn't exist in `public/`. Replaced with a generated `opengraph-image.tsx` using `next/og`.

Verified with `next build` (all 20 routes compile/prerender cleanly) and a local production server — confirmed correct titles, working FAQ anchor, 200s on all routes, and a complete sitemap. Note: the harness's own dev server on `:50109` never responded during this session (unrelated orchestration process, not something in this repo), so final verification was done via a local build/serve.

One out-of-scope item worth flagging: `npm install` reports `next@14.2.5` has a known critical CVE — not something I touched since it's a version-pin decision, but worth a deliberate upgrade call.
